title: Add WebSocket-Based Real-Time Updates
problem: Dashboard relies on polling (15s for telemetry, 10s for agents) creating unnecessary API calls, stale data windows. Users see data up to 15 seconds old.
approach: Implement SSE or WebSocket connections via Next.js API route. Server pushes updates only when state changes. Use event emitter on backend triggered on file-system watch of orchestrator state files.

title: Persistent Storage with SQLite Instead of JSON Files
problem: Orchestrator stores tasks, blackboard, swarms as flat JSON files with race conditions, doesn't scale past ~1000 tasks, no transactional guarantees.
approach: Replace JSON with better-sqlite3. Create tables for tasks, swarms, blackboard_entries with indexes. Wrap mutations in transactions. Add pagination to GET endpoint.

title: Authentication and Access Control
problem: Dashboard and all API routes are completely unauthenticated. Anyone can view status, dispatch tasks, modify blackboard.
approach: Add middleware-based auth with Bearer token. Store hashed ADMIN_TOKEN in env vars. Add minimal login page with httpOnly cookie. Protect all API routes.

title: Historical Telemetry Dashboard with Time-Series Charts
problem: History state only stores data for current session (max 30 points). No way to see uptime trends, latency degradation, or when services went down.
approach: Persist telemetry snapshots server-side in SQLite. Add /api/telemetry/history endpoint with time range queries. Build Recharts-based time-series view.

title: Alerting System with Telegram/Discord Notifications
problem: No push notifications when service goes down or task fails. Must be actively watching dashboard.
approach: Add alerting system that triggers on service status change. Integrate with Telegram/Discord webhooks. Add severity levels and escalation rules.

title: Task Dependencies and DAG Scheduling
problem: All tasks run independently with no dependency tracking. Can't express 'task B should run after task A completes' or handle partial failures in chains.
approach: Add task dependency system with DAG (directed acyclic graph). Implement 'run_after' field referencing parent task ID. Add status propagation (blocked/running/completed/failed).

title: Resource Usage Monitoring (CPU/Memory per Service)
problem: Railway provides no native resource monitoring. Can't see CPU/memory trends or detect memory leaks without external tools.
approach: Add Railway Metrics API integration. Store resource snapshots alongside health checks. Add resource trend charts. Alert on unusual patterns.

title: Automated Rollback on Failed Deploy
problem: When a deployment fails, manual intervention needed to rollback or fix. No automated recovery mechanism.
approach: Add health check before marking deployment success. If service doesn't become healthy within 5min, auto-trigger rollback via Railway API.

title: Self-Healing Automation Scripts
problem: Currently manual intervention needed for common issues like restarting failed workers, clearing stuck queues.
approach: Add configurable healing actions (restart service, clear cache, run migration). Trigger automatically based on health check patterns.

title: Mobile-Optimized Dashboard View
problem: Dashboard not responsive enough for mobile. Can't monitor infrastructure on the go.
approach: Add mobile-first responsive design. Show critical status indicators prominently. Add touch-friendly interactions.
